methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
HLabs is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Paul Hetherington that has raised $5M+. The valuation analysis draws from a broader robotics and manufacturing comp universe of nine private firms and one public company, though narrowly comparable peers at identical or adjacent stages remain limited.

Stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable valuation lens here. Using the $5M total raised as a proxy baseline and applying a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier from Seed to the next funding stage yields an implied valuation of $12M. Peer-median triangulation was not executable; while eleven robotics and manufacturing companies exist in the broader dataset, zero same-stage peers, zero adjacent-stage peers, and zero similar-valuation-tier comps met the minimum three-datapoint threshold, making sector-wide medians unreliable without introducing material bias.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $11M to $14M, with a median point of $12M. This range reflects a conservative 15% discount to high case and a 12% discount to low case, accounting for stage-level uncertainty. Key limitations include the absence of tightly comparable seed-stage peers and reliance on a single methodological approach. Any refinement would require either additional same-stage private comparables in robotics or revenue/unit economics data to support alternative methodologies.
